subject: CLI command name
truth: The legacy version installs the CLI tool `bloodhound-python` while the CE version installs `bloodhound-ce-python`.
quote: The BloodHound.py Legacy installation will add a command line tool `bloodhound-python` to your PATH.

subject: BloodHound.py branch compatibility
truth: The master branch of BloodHound.py is only compatible with BloodHound 4.2 and 4.3; for BloodHound CE you must use the bloodhound-ce branch.
quote: The code in this branch is **only compatible with BloodHound 4.2 and 4.3**. For BloodHound CE, check out the [bloodhound-ce branch](https://github.com/dirkjanm/BloodHound.py/tree/bloodhound-ce)

subject: bloodhound.py package name
truth: The pip-installable package is now named 'bloodhound-ce' instead of 'bloodhound'.
quote: setup(name='bloodhound-ce', version='1.9.0', description='Python based ingestor for BloodHound Community Edition')

subject: output JSON meta version
truth: Output JSON files now write meta version 6 instead of version 5.

subject: BloodHound.py package name
truth: The legacy BloodHound.py is installed via `pip install bloodhound` and the CE version via `pip install bloodhound-ce`; they cannot coexist without virtual environments.
quote: You can only have one of the two tools installed at the same time, unless you use a virtual environment for both tools, or a package manager like pipx that automatically sets these up.
